title: Phase 4: Engine primitives + rule interpreter until 68/68 goldens pass
description: Data-driven rule interpreter per docs/04-scoring-engine.md: ~15 math primitives, dispatch loop over ToolRule/PackageRule/ProfileRule/InsightRule data, gender-split PZSD norms, scopes mcs/pro.person|role|org/insights/reflections. Done when all 68 golden masters reproduce exactly.
notes: Terminology correction (2026-07-10): the close reason above says 'PROD 3.5 config' — that's legacy vendor branding. Corrected framing: the engine implements all 26 primitives the current live product (ProductCatalog code VC18) invokes; no product name is hardcoded in the engine (see ProductCatalog.php, docs/04, docs/07).
close_reason: Engine ported: data-driven interpreter (RuleInterpreter + LegacyConfig + SessionState + ResultAssembler) implementing all 26 primitives the live PROD 3.5 config invokes. 68/68 golden masters reproduce exactly in BOTH keys and strings formats (goldens:verify + GoldenMasterTest). Legacy quirks preserved and documented in CLAUDE-NOTES.

title: Phase 3: Golden-master test harness (build BEFORE engine)
description: Replay harness for the 68 golden sessions in platform/restore-db/goldens/ per docs/10-verification.md. Diffs engine output vs legacy results_keys/strings.json + outstrings.csv. Goldens contain PII - never committed.
close_reason: goldens:verify command + GoldenMaster PHPUnit suite: finds all 68 sessions, per-session/per-field diff vs results_keys.json, points at legacy outstrings.csv on mismatch, exit-code gated. Reports SKIP until engine (phase 4) exists.

title: P7: panel admin API endpoints
description: Laravel /panel/api/* (Sanctum, role-gated): dashboard stats (calls/day, scores by scope, error rates), api-keys CRUD (create/rotate/revoke, rate limits, default code, webhook), access codes + royalty terms CRUD + usage/royalty statements (derivative excluded from totals), assessments search + detail + audit, norm sets list/samples/drift/impact/promote/retire, content browse (read-only), pipeline data from live rule tables.

title: P7: panel scaffold + Sanctum auth
description: React SPA in panel/ (Vite + TS + Tailwind), Laravel Sanctum session auth with admin|viewer roles, panel admin API namespace, login flow, layout shell with nav for the six docs/08 views.

title: P6: promote/retire lifecycle
description: norms:promote / norms:retire commands enforcing the decided policy: >=400/scale threshold for candidate eligibility, promotion is always a manual Arnold action on the impact report, never automated. Retired sets remain queryable; transitions logged.

title: P6: candidate build + impact report
description: norms:build-candidate command: compute raw->cumulative-proportion tables from accumulated samples (smooth sparse tails), create candidate set with provenance. Impact report: rescore recent assessments under candidate vs active, publish % whose top-3 codes change; stored on the set.

title: P6: sample accumulation + drift analytics
description: Anonymized raw PZSD scale-score distributions accumulated per language/gender/scale on every scoring call (aggregate counts, no PII); drift metric vs active norm set for panel display. docs/06.

title: P6: API support for versioned norm sets
description: norms=<norm_set_id> accepted on score calls (active sets; candidate allowed only explicitly for impact testing); provisional flag in envelope; reference/languages norm status read from norm_sets table; GET /v2/reference/norm-sets.

title: P6: engine norm-set resolution
description: convertPzsd/calibratePzsd resolve their conversion matrix through the requested norm set instead of hardcoded gender lookup: male-legacy/female-legacy map to legacy PZSDConversionMatrix rows (goldens must stay 68/68), versioned sets load norm_set_entries.

title: P6: norm_sets schema + seed legacy sets
description: norm_sets + norm_set_entries tables/models per docs/06: slug, status (candidate|active|retired), population filter (language/gender), provenance (n, composition, source range, created_from), entries = (scale, raw, value) conversion rows. Seed male-legacy + female-legacy verbatim from migrated PZSDConversionMatrix (golden fidelity).

title: P5: reference endpoints (languages, questions, translations, scopes)
description: GET /v2/reference/languages (+content coverage + norm status), /questions?tool=&language= (from migrated Question/QuestionTROut), /translations?language= (key->string maps), /scopes (scope -> required tools -> output fields, self-documenting).
close_reason: Built and tested: 34 tests pass incl. golden-gated E2E (envelope reproduces legacy results exactly, both formats; partial pro.role scope works standalone; derivative code meters zero fees).

title: P5: access-code enforcement + usage metering
description: Scoring request carries access_code (or key default); enforce requested scopes subset of code allowed scopes (403 with explanation); enforce max_uses/expires/active. Record usage_event on every scoring call with code_type + fees_due computed from active royalty_terms at event time (multiple terms per code, docs/07). Promote ProductCatalog royalties placeholder to royalty_terms rows.
close_reason: Built and tested: 34 tests pass incl. golden-gated E2E (envelope reproduces legacy results exactly, both formats; partial pro.role scope works standalone; derivative code meters zero fees).

title: P5: one-shot POST /v2/score
description: Convenience endpoint: registration + tools + scopes in one call, sync scoring. Same envelope + validation semantics as the assessment flow.
close_reason: Built and tested: 34 tests pass incl. golden-gated E2E (envelope reproduces legacy results exactly, both formats; partial pro.role scope works standalone; derivative code meters zero fees).

title: P5: assessment endpoints (create, submit tools, score, status, results)
description: POST /v2/assessments, PUT /v2/assessments/{id}/tools/{tool}, POST /v2/assessments/{id}/score {scopes,format,language,norms,access_code,audit}, GET /v2/assessments/{id} status, GET /v2/assessments/{id}/results re-render. Result envelope {assessment_id, external_id, scored_at, language, format, norms:{set_id,provisional?}, scopes:{...}}. Incremental submission; Idempotency-Key + external_id support.
close_reason: Built and tested: 34 tests pass incl. golden-gated E2E (envelope reproduces legacy results exactly, both formats; partial pro.role scope works standalone; derivative code meters zero fees).

title: P5: auth middleware + API key management
description: Authorization: Bearer <api_key>, hashed at rest (sha256 lookup), per-key rate limits, artisan command to issue/revoke keys. Legacy error taxonomy E01 auth.
close_reason: Built and tested: 34 tests pass incl. golden-gated E2E (envelope reproduces legacy results exactly, both formats; partial pro.role scope works standalone; derivative code meters zero fees).

title: P5: tool validation layer (docs/02 rules, structured errors)
description: Validate each tool's responses on write per docs/02 table: counts (27/27/63/96/72/54/54/54/28), ranges, 3x3/3x2 rank constraints for motivators+missions, personalstyle 24 groups of 4 (one 1, one -1, two 0), reflections free text. Errors as array of {tool,q,rule,expected,got}.
close_reason: Built and tested: 34 tests pass incl. golden-gated E2E (envelope reproduces legacy results exactly, both formats; partial pro.role scope works standalone; derivative code meters zero fees).

title: P5: scope resolver + modular scoring output
description: Scope -> required tools per docs/04 dependency matrix (mcs.m/c/s, mcs, pro.role/org/person, insights, reflections, full). score with unmet requirements -> 422 listing missing tools per scope. Assembler filters result envelope to requested scopes. Engine must tolerate partial tool sets for standalone scopes (pro.role/pro.org).
close_reason: Built and tested: 34 tests pass incl. golden-gated E2E (envelope reproduces legacy results exactly, both formats; partial pro.role scope works standalone; derivative code meters zero fees).

title: P5: DB schema + models (api_keys, assessments, access_codes, royalty_terms, usage_events)
description: Migrations + Eloquent models for the API layer per docs/05+07: api_keys (hashed at rest, rate limit, default access code, webhook config), assessments (registration + external_id), assessment_tools (per-tool responses, validated flag), scored_results (norm_set id recorded per docs/06), access_codes (type training|bizdev|derivative, product_code, allowed scopes, max_uses/expires, active), royalty_terms (many per code, kind/amount/recipient/effective window), usage_events (api_key, code, code_type, scopes, fees_due list), idempotency_keys.
close_reason: Built and tested: 34 tests pass incl. golden-gated E2E (envelope reproduces legacy results exactly, both formats; partial pro.role scope works standalone; derivative code meters zero fees).

title: De-brand codebase: remove TAI/PRO-D references, add ProductCatalog
description: TAI is the legacy vendor that owned the IP; PRO-D/Professional Development was one of their report products. Neither should be baked into the new engine's naming, schema, or docs (historical exploration docs/notes are exempt). Also: engine was hardcoded to one product/version-bundle — added ProductCatalog so the product is an access-code catalog entry with support for multiple royalty terms per code (docs/07).
acceptance_criteria: taicode/ renamed to platform/; no TAI/PROD branding in api/, README, compose, CI, or forward-looking docs; engine takes a productCode param instead of hardcoded version constants; 68/68 goldens still pass on both sqlite and MySQL.
close_reason: taicode/ renamed to platform/ (git mv, history preserved) incl. superseded spec file rename; all TAI/PRO-D branding scrubbed from api/, README, compose.yaml, CI, and forward-looking docs (historical exploration docs/notes and the frozen superseded spec are exempt, per instruction). Introduced ProductCatalog: engine takes a productCode param instead of hardcoded version constants, catalog supports multiple royalty terms per code (docs/07 updated). 68/68 goldens re-verified on both sqlite and renamed MySQL instance after the refactor.

title: Phase 5: API layer + access codes
description: Endpoints per docs/05-api-design.md (sync scoring default, optional webhooks, OpenAPI 3.1) + access-code/royalty metering per docs/07 (training/bizdev/derivative code types; Stripe later).
close_reason: Phase 5 complete: all 11 child issues shipped. v2 API layer per docs/05 (auth, assessments, incremental tools, à la carte scope scoring, one-shot, re-render, reference endpoints, idempotency, rate limits, audit trace, webhooks, OpenAPI 3.1) + access codes/royalty metering per docs/07. 36 tests incl. golden-gated E2E.

title: Phase 2: Migration seeds from restore-db/extracted CSVs
description: Migrations + seeders loading rule/matrix/norm/config data from platform/restore-db/extracted/*.csv (pipe-delimited). Per docs/01 + docs/04. Seed data itself stays out of git.
close_reason: legacy:import artisan command: 76 config tables / 53,518 rows from restore-db/extracted, schema inferred, legacy names verbatim, manifest verification (sys.partitions counts are approximate — extract file is truth). WebServiceUsers excluded (live creds).

title: Phase 1: Repo + CI skeleton (Laravel 11 + MySQL 8 + React)
description: Scaffold per docs/09-architecture-deployment.md: Laravel 11 API app, MySQL 8, React control panel placeholder, CI pipeline, repo layout.
close_reason: Laravel 12 (not 11 — EOL security, see README deviation note) scaffolded in api/, GitHub Actions CI (pint + phpunit + conditional golden suite), panel/ stub, monorepo README.

title: P7: under-the-hood pages — pipeline + scope reference + content
description: Pipeline page documenting the 4-stage cascade rendered from LIVE rule data (not hand-maintained), scope reference from /v2/reference/scopes with question counts, content browser (rule sets, translations, question text per language, read-only).

title: P7: views — norms & analytics
description: Active/candidate/retired sets with provenance, sample-size accumulation per language (progress to 400/scale threshold), drift charts, candidate impact reports, promote/retire actions (manual policy).

title: P7: views — dashboard, keys, codes/royalties
description: Dashboard (calls/day, scores by scope, error rate), Clients & API keys (issue/rotate/revoke, limits, webhook config), Access codes & royalties (issue/bulk/revoke, terms mgmt, usage by code type, statement export, derivative visible but excluded from totals). Explainer panel on each view (docs/08 under-the-hood requirement).

title: P7: views — assessments + audit trace UI
description: Search by external_id/email/date; per-assessment status (tools received, scopes scored, results); audit trace walkthrough UI over GET results/audit: rules fired per stage, intermediate scale values, norm set, content keys.

title: P5: OpenAPI 3.1 spec + docs UI
description: Auto-generated OpenAPI 3.1 published at /openapi.json + Scalar (or Swagger UI) docs page per docs/05.
close_reason: Hand-authored OpenAPI 3.1 at /openapi.json (scope/tool enums generated from enforced constants so contract can't drift) + Scalar docs UI at /docs.

title: P5: audit trace (engine hook + endpoint)
description: Optional audit:true on score + GET /v2/assessments/{id}/results/audit per docs/03: rules fired in order, intermediate scale values per stage, norm set + version, content keys resolved. Backs control panel under-the-hood requirement (docs/08).
close_reason: AuditCollector threaded through RuleInterpreter (opt-in, zero cost when off): rules fired in cursor order, per-stage scale values, content keys resolved; stored on scored_results.audit, served at GET results/audit. E2E-tested against a golden session.

title: P5: webhooks (scored/failed, HMAC-signed, per key)
description: Optional per-key webhook URL + secret; deliver scored/failed events HMAC-signed; retries/backoff; delivery log.
close_reason: Per-key webhook_url/secret; scored event HMAC-SHA256-signed, queued with 5 tries + backoff, delivery log in webhook_deliveries. 'failed' event omitted deliberately: scoring is synchronous so failures surface as HTTP errors to the caller.

title: DECISION: Non-binary/unspecified gender norm policy
description: Non-binary/unspecified gender respondents score against pooled-v1 norms (same set already used for clients that can't collect gender). Backfilled record — decided 2026-07-11 during norms policy discussion, alongside prolog-e27. See platform/docs/06-norms.md.
close_reason: Decided 2026-07-11: use pooled-v1 norms for non-binary/unspecified gender respondents.

title: DECISION: Fee schedules per code type + royalty statement cadence
description: Set fee schedules per access-code type (training/bizdev/derivative) and decide royalty statement cadence (monthly/quarterly/etc). See platform/docs/07-access-codes-royalties.md.

title: DECISION: Validation Report 3.0 — commission timing
description: Decide when to commission Validation Report 3.0. The v2 analytics layer (platform/docs/06-norms.md) will collect exactly the data a new report needs, so timing likely follows analytics-layer maturity. Existing evidence has real gaps (see platform/docs/11-products-roadmap.md §6 for full digest: missing alphas, CFA/varimax contradiction, weak scales, no test-retest, no gender invariance/DIF testing, zero non-English validation).

title: DECISION: Enneagram Map content plan
description: Who writes the type content for the Enneagram Map derivative product; also need to reconcile the two non-canonical 'need' labels currently in the source material. See platform/docs/11-products-roadmap.md §2 and platform/docs/12-open-questions.md.

title: DECISION: carry MCS Development into v2 as product/scope preset?
description: Open item from docs/12 + CLAUDE-NOTES: MCS-Dev is a complete dormant second package (PV28/VC19), revive = config/QA only. Decide whether v2 ships it as a scope preset.
close_reason: Decision: carry MCS Development forward as a v2 scope preset. No dedicated build needed now — engine is data-driven (scopes already define required inputs per 04-scoring-engine.md), so MCS Development will be registered as a product/access-code preset as-needed alongside other products, not as a separate upfront project.

title: DECISION: norm sample-size threshold + promotion policy sign-off
description: Open item from docs/06 + docs/12: confirm >=300-400/scale threshold and human-promotion policy for candidate->active norm sets. Needs Arnold sign-off.
close_reason: Decision: candidate norm-set threshold = >=400 responses/scale (not 300, conservative given legacy tables are already thin per split). Promotion candidate->active is always a manual sign-off by Arnold on the auto-generated impact report; never auto-promoted.

title: Phase 8: pooled-v1 norms + docs polish
description: Derive pooled-v1 norms empirically from 16.7k historical sessions, impact report by rescoring history, publish docs per docs/06 + 00-INDEX build order step 8.

title: Phase 6: Norm analytics + versioned norm sets
description: Versioned norm sets (candidate/active/retired), norms param (male|female|pooled|<id>), drift analytics, new-language pipeline per docs/06-norms.md.

title: Phase 7: Control panel (React)
description: API key management, per-key stats/rate limits, under-the-hood self-documentation requirement, audit trace UI per docs/08-control-panel.md.

title: DECISION: Standalone Role Report as a v2 product/scope preset?
description: Role Report data already exists in the launch product (pro.role scope, 54 Qs, per 04-scoring-engine.md scoring scopes table). Standalone packaging is a config/access-code decision, not an engine build. See platform/docs/11-products-roadmap.md §5 and platform/CLAUDE-NOTES.md.
close_reason: Decision: same treatment as MCS Development — pro.role scope already exists (04-scoring-engine.md), so standalone Role Report is a config/access-code registration, not an engine build. Register as a product/preset as-needed, no dedicated project.

title: DECISION: Git hosting + repo creation
description: Repo hosting/creation was an open blocker item — resolved. Repo exists at github.com/arnoldsmyth/pro-logic-scoring-engine. Backfilled record. See platform/docs/09-architecture-deployment.md and platform/docs/12-open-questions.md.
close_reason: Confirmed 2026-07-11: repo already exists and is hosted at github.com/arnoldsmyth/pro-logic-scoring-engine; item was stale.

title: DECISION: rename GitHub repo + local folder off 'pro-d-scoring-engine'?
description: Repo/folder name 'pro-d-scoring-engine' bakes the PRO-D report name into the platform's top-level identity — same issue just corrected inside the codebase. Not done automatically: renaming the GitHub repo changes the remote URL (GitHub redirects, but existing local clones need updating), and beads' issue-id prefix (pro-d-scoring-engine-*) is derived from the repo name at init — renaming would need care to avoid breaking issue-id references already in git history/commit messages. Needs Arnold's call on both the new name and whether to re-key beads.
close_reason: Repo and local folder renamed to pro-logic-scoring-engine (remote confirmed: github.com/arnoldsmyth/pro-logic-scoring-engine). Beads issue-id prefix left as-is to avoid rewriting history.
